seoTitle: How to Integrate Jira and ServiceNow Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Jira and ServiceNow, sync incidents with issues, configure field mapping, and streamline IT and dev collaboration.
intro: IT support and development teams often work in separate systems, which slows down incident resolution. This guide covers connecting Jira and ServiceNow, syncing incidents with issues, and configuring field mapping so both teams stay in sync.

steps.heading: Review the integration overview
steps.body: Understand how the Jira and ServiceNow integration connects incidents on one side with issues on the other.

steps.heading: Connect Jira and ServiceNow
steps.body: Set up the connection between both platforms using the appropriate integration app or connector.

steps.heading: Configure sync and field mapping
steps.body: Map fields between ServiceNow incidents and Jira issues so data flows correctly between the two systems.

steps.heading: Test the integration
steps.body: Create a test incident or issue to confirm data syncs correctly in both directions before rolling it out to your team.

faq.question: How do I integrate Jira and ServiceNow?
faq.answer: Set up the connector between the two platforms, configure field mapping, and test the sync before using it with real incidents.

faq.question: Can incidents in ServiceNow automatically create Jira issues?
faq.answer: Yes, once configured, the integration can automatically sync ServiceNow incidents with corresponding Jira issues.

faq.question: Who typically sets up the Jira ServiceNow integration?
faq.answer: IT administrators and DevOps engineers usually handle the setup since it involves configuring both platforms' settings.

faq.question: Why connect Jira and ServiceNow?
faq.answer: Connecting the two systems improves collaboration between IT support and development teams and streamlines incident resolution.
